title: 10 best herbal tinctures for period cramps
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for period cramps are concentrated liquid extracts of plant-based ingredients that help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The best herbal tinctures for period cramps include Vitex agnus-castus, Paeonia lactiflora, Cimicifuga racemosa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Angelica archangelica, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica sinensis, Hypericum perforatum, and Trifolium pratense. If left untreated, period cramps can significantly impact daily life, causing missed work or school, decreased productivity, and strained relationships due to irritability and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect on the body. The bioactive ingredients, such as angelic acid and isoangelicin, help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions that cause severe pain during menstruation. Additionally, angelica's antispasmodic properties soothe crampy abdominal discomfort, allowing women to find relief from the physical symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Herbal angelica tinctures contain compounds like apiol and asarone, which have been shown to relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from period cramps.

Anti-inflammatory property: Angelica's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterus, ovaries, and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to period pain and discomfort.

Pain-relieving property: The volatile oils present in angelica tinctures, such as alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to reduce pain perception and alleviate menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps alleviate period cramps by relaxing uterine muscles and reducing prostaglandin levels, which contribute to cramping.
Butylphthalide: As a sesquiterpene lactone, butylphthalide has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramp severity and discomfort.
Coclaurine: This phenolic alkaloid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to alleviate period cramps by blocking pain pathways in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a compound called bergapten, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate menstrual cramps.
Leaves: They contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help to reduce period cramps.
Stems: They contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and lignans, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate menstrual cramps and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica archangelica roots and stems in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight to create the tincture.
Allow the tincture to steep in a dark glass jar for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove plant particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon tinctures helps with period cramps because its unique compound, cinnamaldehyde, has a natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. When taken internally as a tincture, it can help to ease the contraction of uterine muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramp discomfort. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties may also help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to painful periods. As a result, incorporating herbal Ceylon cinnamon tinctures into one's wellness routine can offer a natural and effective way to alleviate period cramps and promote overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon tincture contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with menstrual cramps.

Antioxidant properties: The tincture is rich in antioxidants, including polyphenols, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress that may exacerbate period cramp symptoms.

Pain-relieving properties: Ceylon cinnamon contains a compound called cinnamic acid, which has analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, helping to reduce the perception of pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce prostaglandin levels associated with menstrual cramps.
Catechin: A type of flavonoid, catechin has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which could provide relief from period cramps.
Coumarin: A phenolic compound, coumarin has been suggested to have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which may help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamomum verum leaves are used due to their high content of eugenol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps alleviate menstrual cramps.
Barks: Cinnamomum verum barks are used for their warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce period cramp pain.

Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum verum rhyzomes are used due to their high content of cinnamaldehyde, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of cinnamomum verum bark at peak ripeness with a sharp knife in late summer.
Dry the harvested bark in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Combine 250 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the body. The saponins present in licorice root help to relax uterine muscles, reducing contractions and alleviating pain associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, licorice has been shown to increase prostaglandin levels, which can help to stimulate blood flow and reduce inflammation in the uterus, further providing relief from period cramps. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to alleviate discomfort during this time of month.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to menstrual cramping.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agents, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus that can exacerbate period cramps.
Saponins: Help relax smooth muscle tissue, including uterine muscles, thereby decreasing spasms and alleviating menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Licoricidin: Licoricidin, a triterpenoid saponin in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce menstrual pain.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin in Glycyrrhiza glabra, can help reduce the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to menstrual cramps and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties to help ease menstrual cramps.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, providing relief from period cramps.
Barks: The bark of the plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menstrual discomfort, as it contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Collect 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and rinse with distilled water for 10 minutes.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 20 minutes.
Combine 250 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol with the powder in a clean glass jar for 2 hours.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days with occasional shaking for 5 minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a glass bottle with a dropper cap.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony tinctures helps with period cramps because its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including falcarinol and glycosides, has a potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effect. The tincture's ability to relax uterine muscles reduces spasms and cramping, providing relief from intense pelvic pain. Additionally, the herbal formula helps to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause contractions and further exacerbate discomfort. By addressing both physical and hormonal imbalances, Chinese peony tinctures offer a natural approach to alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chinese Peony tincture helps to relax and calm uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstruation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to period cramps.
Pain-relieving property: Chinese Peony tincture contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the production of pain mediators, such as prostaglandins, which are involved in menstrual cramping.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help alleviate period cramp pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain transmission.
Paeoniflorin: A glycoside compound, paeoniflorin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.

Paeonol: A phenolic compound, paeonol has analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and calm uterine spasms associated with period cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Paeonia lactiflora roots are used due to their high concentration of paeonol and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: Paeonia lactiflora flowers are used because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce menstrual cramp pain and promote relaxation.
Leaves: Paeonia lactiflora leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate menstrual cramp pain and reduce inflammation in the uterine muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of paeonia lactiflora roots and 2 parts of flowers fresh or dried in early spring.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with flowers in a clean glass jar.
Add 60 to 80 percent vodka as solvent to the jar covering the herbal material completely.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains a natural compound called casticin, which has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When taken orally, casticin is believed to interact with the body's hormonal systems, reducing prostaglandins, the hormone-like substances responsible for causing uterine contractions and cramping. By regulating prostaglandin production, chaste tree tinctures can provide relief from period cramps, making it a natural and effective solution for managing menstrual discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chasteberry tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce prostaglandins, which are hormones responsible for causing painful cramping during menstruation.

Oxytocin-regulating properties: Chasteberry's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to regulate oxytocin levels, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing the frequency and intensity of uterine contractions.

Hormonal balancing properties: Chaste tree tincture has been traditionally used to balance hormonal fluctuations, including those that can lead to menstrual cramps, by regulating the production of progesterone and estrogen.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They help with period cramps by acting as natural anti-spasmodics, reducing muscle contractions in the uterus that cause pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Vitex agnus castus have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Diterpenes (e.g., aucubin): Diterpenes in the plant may have a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the uterus, reducing spasms and cramping.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high concentration of flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and hormonal balancing properties.
Leaves: Leaves are often used to make tinctures for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Seeds: Seeds of Vitex agnus castus are also used to make tinctures, as they contain bioactive compounds that may help regulate hormonal imbalances and alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh or dried vitex agnus castus flowers and leaves in the summer months.
Chop the vitex agnus castus flowers and leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears.
Combine 1 part vitex agnus castus with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Canada thistle tinctures helps with period cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of uterine contractions. The herb contains a compound called saponin, which has been shown to inhibit prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions in the uterus and lead to cramping pain. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the aerial parts of cirsium arvense in the flowering stage when it is dry.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp blade to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part chopped plant material with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cirsium arvense contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate period cramp symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part fresh taraxacum officinale roots and 2 parts fresh taraxacum officinale leaves in a clean glass jar.
Combine the gathered taraxacum officinale plant material with 70 proof ethanol at a 1:3 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Label and store the taraxacum officinale tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of roots of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated and dry them in a dark place.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedy_parts: Roots: Valeriana officinalis roots are the most commonly used part for making tinctures to alleviate period cramps due to their high valerenic acid content, which has sedative and anti-spasmodic properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part of dried valeriana officinalis roots with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately one ounce per cup.
Combine the chopped herbs with vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar filling it three-quarters full.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks shaking the jar daily to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at a dosage of 20-30 drops per serving taken three times daily.

Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Add 100 proof vodka or a similar high-proof spirit at a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent and stir well.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days with occasional shaking and then strain through cheesecloth into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years or in the refrigerator for longer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with period cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint oil binds to pain receptors in the uterus, reducing inflammation and contractions that cause cramping. Additionally, peppermint's natural muscle-relaxing properties help ease uterine spasms, providing rapid relief from menstrual discomfort. As a result, herbal peppermint tinctures can provide effective and gentle relief for women experiencing painful periods, allowing them to manage their symptoms naturally and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and camphor, which help to reduce inflammation in the uterine lining and alleviate cramp-related discomfort.
Muscle relaxants: The menthol and camphor in peppermint tincture also have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissue, helping to ease uterine contractions and relieve cramping.

Gastrointestinal soothing agents: Peppermint tincture has natural anti-spasmodic properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and alleviate nausea, bloating, and other gastrointestinal symptoms commonly associated with period cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain a high concentration of menthol, which has natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in relieving menstrual cramp discomfort.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains compounds like menthone and limonene, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in late morning or early afternoon for optimal potency.
Combine 250 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container discarding solids.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with period cramps because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort. This soothing effect can help alleviate the intense cramping associated with menstruation, allowing women to find relief from the physical pain. Additionally, lavender's calming properties may also promote relaxation, reducing emotional stress and anxiety often experienced during this time. By addressing both physical and emotional symptoms, English lavender tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for managing period cramps.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender tincture helps to relax muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of menstrual cramps by soothing spasms in the uterine walls.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain caused by inflammation, which can alleviate period cramp discomfort and associated symptoms like bloating and breast tenderness.

Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lavender tincture's calming effects help to reduce anxiety, stress, and mood swings often experienced during menstruation, promoting relaxation and overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lavender roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort and reduce swelling.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, lavender seeds are known for their calming effects on the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and pain associated with period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in their flowering stage and store them in an airtight container for further use.
Combine 250 grams of the harvested flowers with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and label it for identification.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking it daily for 7-10 days and then weekly for the remaining period.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, squeezing the solids gently to extract every last drop of tincture from the plant material.
Transfer the resulting clear liquid to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool dark place for up to five years, shaking well before each use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with period cramps because its active compound, echinacea, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that work to reduce prostaglandins in the uterus. These hormones are responsible for causing contractions and pain during menstruation. The tincture's ability to inhibit prostaglandin production alleviates cramping, allowing women to experience more comfort during their period. Additionally, coneflower's antioxidant properties help soothe inflammation and promote a balanced hormonal response, further reducing the severity of menstrual cramps.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Purple coneflower contains compounds like sesquiterpenes and lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate cramp symptoms.

Pain relief: The tannins and saponins present in purple coneflower tincture have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help numb the pain associated with menstrual cramps and make them more manageable.
Muscle relaxant properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids found in purple coneflower may help relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping that occur during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain and reduce inflammation in the uterine lining.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of purple coneflower possess antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the uterine muscles and ease cramping during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean echinacea purpurea flowers at peak bloom ensuring 100g of dried petals for 4oz tincture.
Chop the dried echinacea petals into small pieces for efficient extraction in a dedicated glass jar.
Mix 1 part chopped echinacea with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a dark glass container sealed tightly for 6 weeks.
Shake the mixture daily for 30 seconds during the 6-week maceration period to facilitate solvent interaction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean amber glass bottle storing it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry tinctures helps with period cramps because of its unique properties that work to alleviate discomfort. The tannins present in barberry help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, providing relief from crampy pain. remedy_recipe: Gather organic barberry roots berberis vulgaris in sufficient quantities.
Dry the barberry roots in a well-ventilated area or with a dehydrator.
Grind the dried barberry roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 part ground barberry root with 2 parts vodka or high-proof liquor and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with period cramps because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscle contractions. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones responsible for causing menstrual cramps. Additionally, St John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the pelvic area. By addressing both the muscular and inflammatory components of period cramps, this herbal tincture can provide effective relief from discomfort and alleviate symptoms associated with PMS.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at peak potency during full bloom.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place or with silica gel for several days.
Combine 1 part dried hypericum perforatum with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks shaking occasionally before straining and filtering.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 2 years.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to period cramps that are treated with herbal tinctures are menstrual migraines, premenstrual syndrome (PMS), and endometriosis symptoms. Herbs like Willow bark, Ginger, and Cramp Bark have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate cramping, pain, and discomfort. Tinctures can also be used to address other gynecological issues such as amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea, and pelvic pain. Herbal remedies provide a natural and gentle approach to managing these symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with period cramps is a blend of cramp bark, ginger, and blue cohosh. Cramp bark is known to ease uterine spasms, while ginger provides anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and discomfort. Blue cohosh, rich in magnesium and other minerals, helps relax the uterine muscles and alleviate cramping. Taking these tinctures individually or in combination, as directed, may help alleviate period cramps and promote overall menstrual well-being. Consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus tinctures helps with period cramps because it promotes hormonal balance, particularly in regulating estrogen and progesterone levels. remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help alleviate period cramps by reducing prostaglandin production, which is a key contributor to the pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in this herb have a potential role in modulating the body's hormonal balance, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate period cramp symptoms.

Seeds: The seeds are occasionally used, as they are thought to have a similar effect on hormone balance, although they are less commonly used than the flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of dried vitex agnus-castus flowers or 4-6 cups of fresh flowers in the summer months.
Combine the harvested flowers with 80% vodka or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow infusion to occur.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to mature before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the body. The active ingredients, such as paeoniflorin and paeonol, interact with the body's prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and pain during menstruation. By regulating prostaglandin levels, Paeonia lactiflora tinctures may help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with PMS, providing relief for women who experience menstrual pain. Regular use may also reduce menstrual cramp severity over time.
remedy_constituents: Paeoniflorin: This glycoside constituent has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that help to alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing prostaglandin levels in the body.
Paeonol: This phenolic constituent has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce menstrual pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Catechin: This flavonoid constituent has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps by scavenging free radicals, reducing inflammation, and relaxing smooth muscle tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Paeonia lactiflora are rich in paeonol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce period cramp pain.
Barks: The barks of Paeonia lactiflora contain paeonol and other flavonoids that help to relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease period cramp discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of Paeonia lactiflora are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that help to relax muscles, reduce inflammation, and alleviate period cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers for herbal tincture preparation.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and dry it in a low-temperature oven at 40c for 2 hours.
Combine the dried plant material with 500ml of 60% ethanol and let it steep for 2-3 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks before straining and filtering it again before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tinctures helps with period cramps because it is rich in phytoestrogens and sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These compounds work by relaxing uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity, and alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome (PMS). remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh cimicifuga racemosa root and 1 cup of fresh cimicifuga racemosa rhizome in late summer or early fall.
Chop the cimicifuga racemosa root and rhizome into small pieces and combine them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80 proof vodka over the cimicifuga racemosa mixture and seal the jar.
Steep the cimicifuga racemosa tincture in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with period cramps because they contain a natural compound called gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to make tinctures for period cramps because they contain high levels of gingerols, compounds known for their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used to make tinctures for period cramps as they contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds, including gingerols, than the mature rhizomes or roots.

Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage and use within 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to soothe and relax the uterine muscles. Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried roots with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts vodka and seal the container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the container every day to combine ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tinctures helps with period cramps because it contains compounds like ligustilide and butylphthalide that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with period cramps because it acts as a natural vasodilator, improving blood flow to the uterus. Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka at a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tinctures helps with period cramps because of its unique combination of compounds that promote relaxation and reduce inflammation. remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with period cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
